reason: The finding requires configuring Rsyslog to authenticate off-loaded audit records, a standard security hardening practice. The provided remediation script automates configuration edits in /etc/rsyslog.conf and /etc/rsyslog.d/, removing legacy entries and ensuring proper authentication mode. This change does not impact SSH/console access, critical services like sshd or sudo, or system boot processes. It aligns with audit security best practices and poses minimal risk of breakage when applied correctly.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; filesystems affected; touches_authn_authz; touches_networking; touches_filesystems
